articles.title: Cargo vessel attacked near Yemen's Hodeidah, UK maritime agency says
articles.summary: Cargo vessel attacked near Yemen's Hodeidah, UK maritime agency says A cargo vessel has come under attack by unidentified armed assailants off Yemen's Red Sea coast, according to the United Kingdom Maritime Trade Operations (UKMTO). The UKMTO said it received a distress call from the vessel while it was around 56km (30 nautical miles) southwest of the Yemeni port city of Hodeidah. The agency said authorities are investigating the incident and advised ships transiting the area to exercise caution and report any suspicious activity. No group has immediately claimed responsibility for the attack.

According to Iran’s foreign ministry, the Hamas delegation, led by Mohammad Darwish, offered condolences and discussed conditions in Gaza and the occupied West Bank, while a Hezbollah delegation headed by senior official Muhammad Fneish conveyed condolences from the group’s leader Naim Qassem. articles.summary: Iraqi cities of Najaf and Karbala to receive Khamenei’s remains The week-long funeral for former Iranian Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei will extend beyond Iran, with ceremonies scheduled in the Iraqi cities of Najaf and Karbala later this week, according to Al Jazeera. After public mourning events in Tehran and a commemoration ceremony in Qom, Khamenei’s remains are expected to be flown to Iraq on Wednesday. articles.summary: Khamenei’s sons attend funeral prayers in Tehran The Associated Press reported that Ali Khamenei’s sons Masoud, Meysam and Mostafa attended funeral prayers for the late Iranian supreme leader in Tehran on Sunday. The ceremony was also attended by Ahmad Vahidi, commander of the Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps. articles.title: Funeral ceremonies continue for Khamenei, senior officials join
articles.summary: Funeral ceremonies continue for Khamenei, senior officials join Funeral prayers were held for Iran’s late Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei on Sunday at Tehran’s Grand Mosalla complex, marking the second day of ceremonies that have drawn large crowds to the capital. President Masoud Pezeshkian, parliament speaker Mohammad Bagher Ghalibaf and other senior officials attended the prayers, which were led by prominent Shia cleric Jafar Sobhani. articles.title: Video: Israeli settlers storm Hebron's Old City under protection of Israeli forces
articles.summary: Video: Israeli settlers storm Hebron's Old City under protection of Israeli forces Illegal Israeli settlers entered Hebron's Old City under the protection of Israeli forces, with footage showing heavily armed soldiers clearing Palestinian residents from streets and public areas to allow the settlers to move through the area. Videos from the scene show settlers roaming the Old City while Israeli forces imposed restrictions on local Palestinian movement. The incident comes amid growing concern over expanding illegal settler activity and tighter Israeli control in Hebron's historic centre. Illega

articles.title: Pakistan to host new round of US-Iran talks, report says
articles.summary: Pakistan to host new round of US-Iran talks, report says Pakistan is expected to host a new round of talks between the United States and Iran on July 11, according to Saudi state-owned broadcaster Al Arabiya, which cited unnamed sources. The report said the discussions are expected to focus on US sanctions on Iran, frozen Iranian assets and Tehran’s nuclear programme. Al Arabiya added that the composition of the Iranian delegation has not yet been finalised and will be decided after the funeral of former Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei.

Another strike killed one more Palestinian and injured several others, some critically, in an Israeli drone attack near the Abu Sharkh roundabout, west of Jabalia refugee camp in the northern Gaza Strip, WAFA reported. articles.title: More than 140 Palestinian citizens of Israel killed this year
articles.summary: More than 140 Palestinian citizens of Israel killed this year More than 140 Palestinian citizens of Israel have been killed so far this year, a 12 percent increase from the same period in 2025, according to data cited by AFP from the Abraham Initiatives, an organisation that promotes coexistence between Jewish and Arab communities. The deaths have been linked largely to criminal gangs, family feuds, widespread access to firearms and what many community members describe as inadequate police enforcement. articles.summary: OPEC+ tipped to raise quotas again as Middle East calms Seven OPEC+ members are likely to raise oil production quotas on Sunday as Gulf countries are reeling from the Middle East war, analysts told AFP. Saudi Arabia, Russia and five other members of the enlarged Organisation of the Petroleum Exporting Countries (OPEC+) are meeting online on Sunday to discuss quotas for August. articles.title: Ship-tracking firm says four ships changed course in Hormuz
articles.summary: Ship-tracking firm says four ships changed course in Hormuz Four vessels attempting to transit the Strait of Hormuz via the route along Oman’s coast have altered course, ship-tracing firm Windward said. In a post on X, Windward said two of the vessels reversed course and the other two diverted to a central corridor. Iran opposes ships using the southern route through Oman’s territorial waters and has previously attacked at least two vessels travelling that corridor.  The attacks triggered retaliatory attacks on Iranian military assets by the US in and around Hormuz.
